The centrosome consists of pericentriolar material, Centrioles, Secretory vesicles, lysosome, smooth endoplasmic reticulum, peroxisome, mitochondrion, and microtubule.](Attachments/Books/biochemistry-for-sport-and-exercise-maclaren/c03f011.png)

**Figure 3.11** The basic structure of body cells

## Definition

The basic structure of a body cell comprises a set of membrane-bound and non-membrane-bound organelles and structural elements that collectively define eukaryotic cellular architecture.

## Core Principles

- Body cells contain surface and projection structures including the flagellum, cilium, and microvilli.
- Genetic and protein-processing components include chromatin, the nuclear pore, the nucleolus, ribosomes, the rough endoplasmic reticulum, the Golgi complex, and the proteasome.
- Energy and metabolic storage/processing structures include glycogen granules, mitochondrion, peroxisome, and smooth endoplasmic reticulum.
- Vesicular and degradative compartments include secretory vesicles and lysosomes.
- The cytoskeleton is composed of microtubules, microfilaments, intermediate filaments, and microvilli.
- The centrosome consists of pericentriolar material and centrioles.

## Sub-concepts

- **Cytoskeleton:** microtubules, microfilaments, intermediate filaments, microvilli.
- **Centrosome:** pericentriolar material, centrioles.
- **Endomembrane system:** rough endoplasmic reticulum, smooth endoplasmic reticulum, Golgi complex, secretory vesicles, lysosomes.
- **Nuclear components:** chromatin, nuclear pore, nucleolus.
- **Energy/metabolic organelles:** mitochondrion, peroxisome, glycogen granules.
- **Surface projections:** flagellum, cilium, microvilli.

## Field-level Controversies

## 文獻分歧 / Discussion

## Practical Applications

Understanding the basic cellular structure underpins interpretation of cellular responses to exercise and metabolic demand, including ATP production (mitochondrion), energy storage (glycogen granules), and protein synthesis/turnover (ribosomes, rough ER, proteasome).
